findings.ruleName: Secret-like Token or Credential In Logging Path
findings.title: Secret-like Token or Credential In Logging Path
findings.explanation: Template field 'rawBody' suggests raw body or payload data is being logged. Raw request/response bodies frequently contain credentials, PII, or secrets.
findings.message: Template field 'rawBody' suggests raw body or payload data is being logged. Raw request/response bodies frequently contain credentials, PII, or secrets.
findings.remediation: Replace '{{ rawBody }}' with individually named safe fields. If body inspection is required for debugging, apply a content-filtering or redaction pipeline before logging.
findings.recommendation: Replace '{{ rawBody }}' with individually named safe fields. If body inspection is required for debugging, apply a content-filtering or redaction pipeline before logging.

findings.ruleName: Structured Log Field Is Not Allowed
findings.title: Structured Log Field Is Not Allowed
findings.explanation: Structured field 'password' is not allowed by the active logging policy. Profile: 'default'.
findings.message: Structured field 'password' is not allowed by the active logging policy. Profile: 'default'.
findings.remediation: Remove the 'password' field from the log call. If this data is needed for debugging, use a Cerbi redaction adapter.
findings.recommendation: Remove the 'password' field from the log call. If this data is needed for debugging, use a Cerbi redaction adapter.

findings.title: Sensitive Data In Log
findings.explanation: Field 'email' is considered sensitive (profile: 'default'). Logging sensitive fields may expose PII or confidential data in log sinks.
findings.message: Field 'email' is considered sensitive (profile: 'default'). Logging sensitive fields may expose PII or confidential data in log sinks.
findings.remediation: Review whether 'email' needs to be logged. Consider masking, hashing, or omitting this field. Use a Cerbi redaction adapter if the field is required for diagnostics.
findings.recommendation: Review whether 'email' needs to be logged. Consider masking, hashing, or omitting this field. Use a Cerbi redaction adapter if the field is required for diagnostics.

findings.title: High Cardinality Logging Field
findings.explanation: Structured field 'email' looks high-cardinality and may increase log index/storage cost.
findings.message: Structured field 'email' looks high-cardinality and may increase log index/storage cost.
findings.remediation: Keep 'email' out of indexed dimensions unless it is explicitly needed for correlation; prefer sampling, hashing, or non-indexed attributes.
findings.recommendation: Keep 'email' out of indexed dimensions unless it is explicitly needed for correlation; prefer sampling, hashing, or non-indexed attributes.

findings.ruleName: Exception Logging May Expose Sensitive Object State
findings.title: Exception Logging May Expose Sensitive Object State
findings.explanation: Destructuring operator used on 'payload' (potentially-sensitive object type). Object destructuring serialises the full object graph into the log, which may expose sensitive fields that are not individually named.
findings.message: Destructuring operator used on 'payload' (potentially-sensitive object type). Object destructuring serialises the full object graph into the log, which may expose sensitive fields that are not individually named.
findings.remediation: Replace '{@payload}' with individually named, safe fields. If the full object is required for diagnostics, apply a Cerbi destructuring policy or use a masking destructuring policy.
findings.recommendation: Replace '{@payload}' with individually named, safe fields. If the full object is required for diagnostics, apply a Cerbi destructuring policy or use a masking destructuring policy.
